title: acaena subtussericascens
intro: Acaena subtussericascens, also known as the hairy caterpillar's toe, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family, native to New Zealand. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Acaena subtussericascens include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Acaena subtussericascens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal benefits.
